description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a single 'build' stage that defines two jobs, auto_package and triggered_package. The auto_package job, marked interruptible and running on runners tagged win10-vs2017 and osgeo4w, executes the script commands: export DELIVERY_ENV=test; echo "Commit branch:$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH"; if [[ $CI_PROJECT_NAMESPACE == \"Oslandia\" ]]; then OSGEO4W_REPO_FOLDER=\"extra\"; else OSGEO4W_REPO_FOLDER=\"extra-$CI_PROJECT_NAMESPACE-$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH\"; fi; echo "OSGeo4W repository folder:$OSGEO4W_REPO_FOLDER"; if [[ \"$CI_COMMIT_BRANCH\" != \"master\" ]]; then echo "Branch build! "; REF_COMMIT=origin/master; else echo "Master build!"; REF_COMMIT=$CI_COMMIT_BEFORE_SHA; export DELIVERY_ENV=release; fi; echo "Use diff $REF_COMMIT...$CI_COMMIT_SHA to get modified packages list"; export PACKAGE_NAMES=$(git diff --name-only $REF_COMMIT...$CI_COMMIT_SHA | grep -oP '^packages/\K[^/]*' | uniq | grep -v __inc__); echo "Following packages has been modified and will be built:"; echo "$PACKAGE_NAMES"; if [ -z "$PACKAGE_NAMES" ]; then echo "No package to build !"; exit 0; fi; ./scripts/packages.sh $DELIVERY_ENV $PACKAGE_NAMES. The triggered_package job runs only for pipeline triggers, uses runners tagged win10 and osgeo4w, stores the output/ directory as an artifact, and runs: cd packages/${PACKAGE_NAME}; if [ -x package.sh ]; then /bin/bash ./package.sh ${DELIVERY_ENV}; else cmd /c "package.cmd ${DELIVERY_ENV}"; fi; cd ../..; scp scripts/genini scripts/gen_setup_ini.sh ci@ci.oslandia.net:~/; ssh ci@ci.oslandia.net ./gen_setup_ini.sh ${DELIVERY_ENV}. No Docker image or services are defined, and the pipeline relies on the provided tags and the only/trigger rule for the second job.
